description: Computes the orthogonal projection of a point onto a line or vector, returning the foot of the perpendicular. The result is the closest point on the target to the given point.
utility: Project a point onto a line returning the foot of the perpendicular

arguments.name: line_or_vec
arguments.description: Line or vector to project onto

alternates.description: Foot of perpendicular from P to line L
Foot of perpendicular from P onto vector V

examples.scenario: Project triangle vertex onto base to find altitude foot in sine law proof
examples.expression: graph_1 = g2d(at(-10, 11.4), size(25), range(-1, 8), grid(noaxes), type(geometry)); triangle_1 = sas(graph_1, 5, 40, 6, point(graph_1, 0, 0)); line_c = line(graph_1, item(triangle_1, type(edge), 1), type(segment), c(blue), defer()); point_c = item(triangle_1, type(vertex), 3); point_projected_d = project(graph_1, line_c, point_c, s(0), fo(0), t(0))

examples.scenario: Draw height line from projected point to vertex
examples.expression: line_height = line(graph_1, point_c, point_projected_d, type(segment), dash(), c(red), notes("This will be the height of the triangle")); label_h = label(graph_1, at(line_height, 3), "h", buff(0, 0.5))

examples.scenario: Mark right angle at the projected foot
examples.expression: point_a = item(triangle_1, type(vertex), 1); angle_right = angle(graph_1, point_projected_d, point_c, point_a, c(blue))

examples.scenario: Project a point onto horizontal ground axis for elevation angle
examples.expression: ground_axis = line(G, viewer, runway_end, c(gray)); ground_projection = project(G, ground_axis, plane_point)

examples.scenario: Project circle center onto a secant line
examples.expression: G=g2d(at(2,12),size(30),range(-7,7),type(geometry)); secant_line = line(G, A, B, c(blue)); foot = project(G, secant_line, center)

examples.scenario: Project a point onto x-axis and y-axis for unit circle trigonometry
examples.expression: cos_foot = project(G, line(G, point(G, -2, 0), point(G, 2, 0)), P); sin_foot = project(G, line(G, point(G, 0, -2), point(G, 0, 2)), P)
